beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check messages while rain patters outside.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus wakes early as usual; yesterday's low energy (35/100) after the crowded bar and evening run means a slower, quieter start. Rain and SR-520 closure will shape the day's routing.

beats.activity: Drive to Renton office via I-90 (avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure); steady rain the whole way.
beats.reasoning: Tax season wrap-up still underway; SR-520 closure forces I-90 route adding ~15 min buffer. Grey drizzle makes for careful driving.

beats.activity: File final tax returns and client reconciliations at desk.
beats.reasoning: End-of-season filings; Cyrus is a perfectionist and tax season is winding down. Morning block before rain clears gives focus time.

beats.activity: Lunch at desk or nearby café—rain still steady, so grab something quick and stay dry.
beats.reasoning: Rain peaks through 2pm; Cyrus avoids outdoor errands. Fuels up before afternoon stretch.

beats.activity: Finish tax season paperwork and client follow-ups; rain clearing by 2pm.
beats.reasoning: Longest work block of the day; Cyrus clears the backlog so he can transition to 'hiker mode' starting tomorrow. Rain subsiding by end of block.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle, taking I-90 westbound (SR-520 closure no longer an issue in this direction); rain mostly done.
beats.reasoning: Heads home with the cleared weather; leaves Renton early enough to beat evening traffic and catch daylight.

beats.activity: Stop at Elliott Bay Book Company to browse new arrivals and chat with staff; yesterday's impulse buy is still on his mind.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus can't resist a bookstore and yesterday's visit left him wanting more. Detour is on the way home and weather is clearing—perfect evening mood.

beats.activity: Drive from Capitol Hill back to Madison Park; pick up fresh groceries at QFC for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Swing through neighborhood grocery store on the way home; low-key errand fits the decompressed post-rain evening.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; listen to music or a podcast while cooking.
beats.reasoning: Community-minded and perfectionist, Cyrus takes care with meals. Quiet evening at home after a steady work day mirrors his low-energy recovery mode.

beats.activity: Read the Cascades memoir picked up yesterday; feet up, cup of tea, early bedtime brewing.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down with the book; Cyrus values quiet time with friends and books over crowds. Yesterday's energy depletion means he's prioritizing rest and reflection.
Full 8-hour recovery sleep; Cyrus rebuilds energy for hiking season. Sleep bar spans overnight as required.

diary: Tuesday was one of those grey, drizzly days that makes you grateful for a warm office and hot coffee. Started the morning listening to rain patter against the windows at home—that particular sound always settles something in me before the day begins. The commute to Renton was slick but manageable; I heard about the SR-520 closure on the radio, glad I'd already routed around it. Spent most of the morning and early afternoon grinding through the last of tax season paperwork—client reconciliations, final returns, all the tedious but necessary work. Grabbed lunch at my desk to stay dry. By mid-afternoon the rain was clearing, so I took the scenic route home via Capitol Hill and stopped by Elliott Bay Book Company. Just browsed for a bit, chatted with the staff about some new hiking guides they'd gotten in. Picked up groceries on the way back to Madison Park, made a simple dinner while listening to a podcast, then settled in with the Cascades memoir I'd impulse-bought yesterday. Feet up, tea in hand, lost in those mountains while the city quieted down outside. Felt peaceful.
thought_process: The rain and road closure kind of set the tone for a slower, more introspective day. I could've let the SR-520 backup frustrate me, but I'd already planned around it—tax season has taught me to anticipate complications. Work felt manageable today, maybe because the end is actually in sight now. I lingered at Elliott Bay instead of rushing home because something in the drizzle made me want to be around books and people, even if just for twenty minutes. The impulse buy yesterday was calling to me. By evening, I was genuinely ready to settle in—no hiking weather today, no energy for anything ambitious. The memoir and tea felt like the right reward for pushing through tax season. My mood shifted from focused-but-tired this morning to genuinely content by nightfall.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
